commit.message:
feat: replace in-tree docs tooling with mkdocs-terok package (#116)

* feat: replace in-tree docs tooling with mkdocs-terok package (#115)

Replace ~1,016 lines of docs generation code with thin shims importing
from the shared mkdocs-terok package. This eliminates drift with terok's
copy and removes a hardcoded Codecov token from gen_quality_report.py.

- Delete docs/ci_map.py, docs/test_map.py, docs/stylesheets/extra.css
- Replace gen_ci_map.py, gen_test_map.py, gen_quality_report.py,
  gen_ref_pages.py with thin shims delegating to mkdocs_terok
- Add gen_brand_css.py shim for shared brand CSS
- Update test_ci_map.py to import from mkdocs_terok.ci_map directly
- Add mkdocs-terok v0.1.0 dependency to pyproject.toml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: strip output_prefix from ref_pages nav entries

The generate_ref_pages() returns full doc paths (e.g.
reference/terok_shield/cli.md), but SUMMARY.md is written inside
reference/, causing literate-nav to resolve double-prefixed paths
(reference/reference/...). Strip the output_prefix so nav entries
are relative to the SUMMARY.md location.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add mkdocs-terok to test dependency group

test_ci_map.py imports from mkdocs_terok.ci_map, so the package must
be available when only the test group is installed (without docs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "fix: add mkdocs-terok to test dependency group"

This reverts commit 2c16712dfd4b439c950298b908fdafe00015bf63.

* fix: remove test_ci_map.py — testing is mkdocs-terok's responsibility

The ci_map logic now lives in the mkdocs-terok package, so its unit
tests belong there too. Keeping them here would require adding
mkdocs-terok as a test dependency just to test another package's
internals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix: /top-picks now returns instantly from cache

Was doing a 30s full scan inline on HTTP request, causing
browser fetch timeout -> HTML error page -> JSON parse error
("Unexpected token '<'").

Now /top-picks ALWAYS returns immediately:
- If cache exists: serve it
- If no cache: return empty with 'scanning' status

Heavy scan moved to _generate_picks(), called only by
background thread. Dashboard auto-refreshes every 30s
so it picks up data as soon as the first scan completes.

feat(tax): Solana token metadata pipeline — Helius DAS + renderTokenBadge

- Add tcmd_token_meta localStorage store: mintAddress, symbol, name,
  imageUrl, decimals, metadataSource, lastResolvedAt, resolved
- resolveTokenMetaBatch(): calls Helius DAS getAssetBatch (100/req),
  falls back to existing DexScreener/Pump.fun namesCache for imageUrl;
  marks unresolved entries with 24h retry TTL
- getTokenMeta(mintOrSym): synchronous O(1) lookup by full mint or
  8-char prefix
- Integrate into resolveAllTokenMetadata pipeline (runs after
  DexScreener/Pump.fun so namesCache is already populated)
- Enrich resolved transactions with imageUrl from DAS store
- renderTokenBadge(sym, name, imageUrl, mint, size): shared helper
  showing icon + symbol + optional name + Solscan link for unresolved
- renderTxRow: uses renderTokenBadge + getTokenMeta for sent/recv cells
- renderReviewRow: token info block now shown for unknown_asset reason
  (not just missing_price/outlier); shows DAS image, full name, metadata
  source badge (Helius DAS ✓ / pump.fun / DexScreener)
- tax-engine.js v16, tax-ui.js v17

commit.message:
Monitor: add 12-hour mute window after well recovery alert

The stagnation-start timestamp returned by _find_recovery_in_data can shift
slightly as new snapshots arrive, causing the dedup comparison to fail and
fire duplicate alerts. Fix by muting recovery alerts for 12 hours after one
fires, regardless of what timestamp the algorithm returns.

commit.message:
Fix post-process pipeline: 429 backoff, isolated DB sessions, periodic commits

Geocoding now detects Nominatim 429s and aborts early after 5 consecutive
failures instead of hammering the API for 20+ minutes. Each pipeline step
uses its own DB session so a geocoding failure no longer cascades to score
and dedup. Periodic commits every 10 geocodes keep the DB connection alive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

MAJOR: Code at &0600 is FDC disc I/O, not IRQ handler

Rich Talbot-Watkins (co-author) identified that the hardware
addresses were wrong. Corrected based on definitive BBC Master
Sheila address map:

  &FE28 = 1770 FDC Status(R)/Command(W) — NOT System VIA IFR
  &FE2A = 1770 FDC Sector register — NOT VIA Timer 2 Low
  &FE2B = 1770 FDC Data register — NOT VIA Timer 2 High

This means the code at &0600 is a disc I/O NMI handler used
during loading, NOT a music/sound IRQ handler:
  - Reads FDC status to check for DRQ (data ready)
  - Transfers bytes from FDC Data register to sideways RAM
  - Advances a destination pointer and decrements byte count
  - Manages sector sequencing for multi-sector reads

The PHA/RTI pattern is correct for an NMI handler (the 6502
NMI vector is called directly, unlike IRQ which goes through
the MOS). Rich also noted the handler doesn't restore A from
&FC, confirming it's NOT an IRQ1V handler.

All labels renamed:
  irq_handler → nmi_disc_handler
  music_handler → fdc_read_byte
  music_ptr_lo/hi → disc_load_ptr_lo/hi
  music_note_reset → disc_load_complete
  music_env_patch → disc_state_patch
  sound_state_block → disc_state_block

System VIA addresses (&FE40-&FE4E) were already correct.
Still byte-exact: 0 mismatches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

WIP: bootable disc image (not yet working)

Added data/ directory with decrypted binary files extracted from the
running game's memory, a BASIC boot loader (boot.bas), and updated
frogman.asm to build a disc image with PUTFILE/PUTBASIC.

The disc builds but doesn't boot correctly yet. The missing piece
is the game's main loop — the ~1KB engine contains only subroutines
(render, sprites, sound), but something must call them in a loop.
This orchestration code is set up by the original encrypted Loader
and we haven't been able to capture it yet.

Investigation found:
- The Loader's 55-stage XOR chain uses VIA timer values as keys,
  making each stage dependent on the decrypted output of the previous
- Patching memory to set breakpoints corrupts the XOR chain
- IRQ1V doesn't point to &0600 as expected — the interrupt hookup
  is more complex than assumed
- Proper jsbeeb MCP breakpoint/register-read support is needed
  to trace the decryption chain to its end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add position monitor, auto-exit, and sell functionality

Position Monitor:
- /position-monitor endpoint: shows all positions with current
  market prices, entry price, unrealized P&L (cents + %)
- Dashboard positions table now shows Entry, Current, P&L columns
- SELL button on each position for instant exit

Auto-Exit System:
- Take profit at +20% (sell when position is up 20%+)
- Stop loss at -15% (cut losses when position is down 15%+)
- Runs every 30s in background loop when auto-trade is ON
- Logs all auto-exits

Sell API:
- /sell-position endpoint: sell existing positions
- Uses action="sell" per Kalshi API v2
- Records all sells in trade history

Settlement filter loosened to 180 days since we can now exit
early via sell — dont need to wait for settlement to compound.

commit.message:
Hard 90-day max on ALL picks — compounding requires fast turns

Every pick (hero, sports, non-sports, misc) must settle within
90 days. No more 9-month or multi-year bets tying up capital.

$73 → $1M requires aggressive compounding. A $5 bet that
returns $6 in 3 days can be reinvested immediately. The same
bet locked up for 9 months is dead money.

Filter out bad risk/reward from hero picks

loop into the script body so all functions land in the caller's scope.

commit.message:
feat: RecaptureDetector v1.12, AF/AE convergence wait e coleta de dataset

## RecaptureDetector v1.9 → v1.12
- v1.9: fix bottleneck detectEmissiveSurface (Long arithmetic + strideEmissive)
- v1.10: tabelas DFT pré-calculadas (4611ms → 213ms → ~60ms)
- v1.11: center crop 128×128 stride=1; remove specular do combined; pesos Moiré 0.5 / Chromatic 0.2
- v1.12: corrige bloqueio matematicamente impossível sem cromático (Moiré 0.55→0.70,
  Chromatic 0.35→0.20); alarga banda DFT 2-15 → 2-70 para cobrir fill de tela 40-90%

## SecureCameraCapture — aguardo de convergência AF/AE
- Camera2Interop CaptureCallback rastreia CONTROL_AF_STATE / CONTROL_AE_STATE
- Adiciona INACTIVE e PASSIVE_UNFOCUSED como estados convergidos (Samsung A16 inicia em INACTIVE)
- settleTimeoutMs calibrado em 1500ms (sessão Camera2 no A16 abre em ~1050ms)
- camera_settle_ms registrado em pipelineTimingsMs
- ResolutionSelector.HIGHEST_AVAILABLE_STRATEGY corrige resolução 640×480 default

## ProvviCapture
- bypassRecaptureBlock: Boolean = false — permite coleta de dataset de recapturas intencionais
- camera_settle_ms incluído em timings e allAssertions

## Demo app — DatasetCollectionScreen
- Preview ao vivo via Flutter camera + hand-off para SDK YUV (250ms delay)
- Sidecar .scores.json salvo junto ao JPEG com scores SDK pré-codec (preserva sinal UV)
- Fix preview distortion (FittedBox + SizedBox expand/cover)

## Diagnostic tool
- Suporte a sidecar: lê .scores.json via ContentResolver antes de reanalisar JPEG
- Coluna source no CSV (sdk_yuv vs reanalysis_jpeg)
- Fix separador decimal locale-dependente no CSV

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Simplify installation to a 3-command quick path

- Add CDN fallback (jsDelivr 0.4.x) in hook_library_info_build() so
  the toolbar JS loads without local library installation; downgrade the
  runtime JS check from REQUIREMENT_ERROR to REQUIREMENT_WARNING
- _instruckt_drupal_iife_available() always returns TRUE now that the
  toolbar library is always registered (local or CDN)
- Downgrade install-phase private stream check from REQUIREMENT_ERROR to
  REQUIREMENT_WARNING so drush en succeeds without file_private_path set
- Guard hook_install() directory creation behind isValidScheme('private')
  to avoid InvalidStreamWrapperException when private:// isn't configured
- Add step 0 to drush instruckt:setup: creates ../private directory,
  appends file_private_path to settings.php, and creates _instruckt/
  storage dirs — all idempotent; inject FileSystemInterface and
  StreamWrapperManagerInterface for this work
- Fix @option docblock trailing periods being parsed by Drush as default
  option values (removed "Default: X." from annotation descriptions)
- Fix instruckt_drupal.info.yml configure: key pointing to nonexistent
  route instruckt_drupal.settings → instruckt_drupal.admin_settings
- Update RequirementsTest to expect REQUIREMENT_WARNING for missing IIFE
- Rewrite README Installation section: 3-step quick path, optional local
  JS section, collapsed details block for manual MCP auth alternative;
  remove private filesystem and composer.json as hard prerequisites

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Run login API on Edge runtime to match middleware token generation

The login was failing because the login API (Node.js runtime) and the
middleware (Edge runtime) were computing different HMAC-SHA256 signatures
from the same secret. The cookie set by login was always rejected by
middleware. Fixed by running the login route on Edge runtime so both
use the same crypto implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: analytics-dashboard
repository.description: Planet Detroit Analytics Dashboard — audience, newsletter, and civic action metrics

commit.message:
fix(functions): hardcode deploy path instead of relying on PSScriptRoot

$PSScriptRoot and $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path are unreliable when
dot-sourced from an interactive session. The dotfiles always deploy to
$HOME\.dotfiles, so the functions directory path is always known.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clear stale agent tracking on turn_duration and new user prompt

When a user cancels/interrupts an Agent tool call, Claude Code never
emits a tool_result for it. This leaves the agent in active_agents
forever, causing the daemon to stay in active/subagent state and
never transition to idle.

- Clear active_agents on turn_duration (turn is definitively over)
- Clear active_agents on new user prompt (cancelled agents are stale)
- Remove active_agents gate from turn_duration idle transition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(setup): reliably rename init branch to main regardless of git default

git init may create 'master' or 'main' depending on system config.
Detect the actual branch name and only rename if it differs from 'main'
rather than silently suppressing the error with 2>$null.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rebuild Loachy as v4: real de-vigged edge detection + all bug fixes

Core strategy fix:
- Replace fake edge formula (avg vig prob vs best prob = meaningless) with
  additive de-vig: normalize avg implied probs per market to get true probability,
  then edge = true_prob - to_prob(best_price). This is real positive-EV detection.
- Require min 2% edge AND min 3 books confirming price (was 1% + 1 book)
- Kelly bet sizing now uses live wallet.cash not fixed paper_budget
- Remove zero-edge fallback candidates entirely (AI only sees real edges)

Bug fixes:
- Overnight skip: 2-6 UTC (was 0-8, cut NFL/NBA primetime windows)
- auto_bet_confidence: 0.72 (was 0.65)
- pending_expiry: 90 min OR game_start-30min, whichever sooner (was 30 min < scan interval)
- MAX_LIVE_CALLS: 4 (was 1, 8/9 sports used stale cache every scan)
- Weather: real team city coordinates + INDOOR_TEAMS skip (was Kansas for every game)
- Score settlement: 30-min cache per sport (saves Odds API credits)
- Streak reduce factor: 0.70 (was 0.50)
- AI model: claude-haiku (cheaper/faster)
- AI prompt: veto-oriented with true_prob%, edge%, book count context
- Parlay edge: uses de-vigged true_prob (was vig-inflated consensus)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(functions): use MyInvocation path instead of PSScriptRoot in main.ps1

$PSScriptRoot is empty when a script is dot-sourced from a profile,
causing $_FunctionsDir to be empty and all function files to silently
skip loading. $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path is always set correctly
when dot-sourcing a .ps1 file.

Fix JS crash: unescaped apostrophe in 'we'll' broke all JS

commit.message:
fix: inject theme instructions into generation prompt

- Add src/lib/themes.ts with 15 theme definitions (layout, typography,
  color usage instructions for each theme)
- API route now imports getThemeInstructions and injects the theme block
  into the user prompt so the AI produces visually distinct HTML per theme
- Removed generic themeDirective string that had no effect
- Normalize underscore theme keys to hyphens for backward compatibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove arbitrary timeout waiting for transcript file

The daemon waited only 5 seconds for the JSONL transcript to appear
before giving up. Sessions with many MCP servers (e.g. imbi with
postgres, clickhouse, neo4j, zoekt) take longer to initialize,
causing the daemon to exit before the transcript is created.

- Remove the 50-attempt (5s) limit on transcript file polling
- Keep polling as long as the parent Claude process is alive
- Clean up normally if the parent process dies

commit.message:
feat(testing): local test infrastructure, profiling, strictness — 2,430 tests, 0 failures

Testing infrastructure:
- scripts/test-all.sh: 3-tier local test runner (fast/full/heavy) replacing CI
- scripts/smoke-test.sh: quick health check for Docker stack
- scripts/profile.sh: master profiling script (pyinstrument, memray, radon, twiggy)
- Deleted pytest.ini (was overriding pyproject.toml), moved config to pyproject.toml
- Excluded test_schema_fuzzing.py from main suite (poisons settings cache)

Profiling & optimization:
- pyinstrument, memray, radon added to dev deps
- rollup-plugin-visualizer for bundle treemap (ANALYZE=1 npx vite build)
- SQLAlchemy slow query profiler middleware (SLOW_QUERY_THRESHOLD_MS env var)
- twiggy + cargo-bloat for WASM size analysis

Config strictness:
- basedpyright include fixed: sleep_scoring_app → sleep_scoring_web
- ruff: re-enabled C901/PLR0912/PLR0915 with per-file ignores for existing outliers
- ESLint: added complexity, max-depth, no-explicit-any, no-console, prefer-const
- Lighthouse: promoted accessibility/FCP/LCP/TBT from warn → error
- pytest: added --strict-config, filterwarnings for DeprecationWarning
- Pre-commit: fixed basedpyright (was || true), added gitleaks, eslint, clippy

CI slimmed:
- Removed 20 GitHub Actions jobs, kept only Chromatic (visual regression on push)
- Deleted ci.yml (duplicate)
- Everything runs locally via scripts/test-all.sh

Bug fixes:
- test_postgres_specific: minute overflow (0-99 → h:m), greenlet after rollback
- test_api_consensus: SQLite race condition in concurrent votes
- test_markers_autoscore: stale state assertion
- test_benchmarks: CSV loader skip_rows=0 for headerless test data
- Zod schemas: derived from OpenAPI constants (no drift), removed 12 dead schemas
- API validation: console.warn → console.error for schema mismatches
- test-all.sh: ((VAR++)) crash under set -e, SITE_PASSWORD leak in ps

Frontend:
- Zod wired: LoginFormSchema + zodResolver, API response validation
- Container structure tests for backend + frontend Docker images
- Property-based tests: 77 Hypothesis tests (marker placement, complexity, metrics, export)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(server): batch extraction tick observations to 1000 rows (#279)

Replace hardcoded LIMIT 10000 with EXTRACTION_BATCH_SIZE constant (1000)
as a named bind parameter in the extraction_tick observation query.
Extract fetch_observation_batch() helper to make the batch/watermark logic
unit-testable without requiring the full embedding pipeline.

Add 6 unit tests verifying: first-batch cap, watermark advancement across
multiple ticks, remainder processing, empty-store behaviour, no
re-processing past watermark, and constant value assertion.
